concerns.analysis:
Page 1 of the source PDF contains an official agency logo ('NC Energy, Mineral & Land Resources Environmental Quality') at the top left. This is a meaningful image that identifies the document's authoring organization. However, the rendered HTML output lacks an `` element for this logo. Instead, it only provides a plain text placeholder inside a paragraph tag (`

[Image: ...]

`). Because there is no functional image element in the DOM, the image is visually missing from the output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The visual logo for 'NC Energy, Mineral & Land Resources Environmental Quality' at the top left of the document.
concerns.output_quote: [Image: Logo for North Carolina Energy, Mineral & Land Resources, Environmental Quality]

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (2 list(s)): p1:letter+roman, p4:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=2. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.

The word 'MEMORANDUM' serving as the primary document title on Page 1 is underlined in the source PDF. The extraction output captures it as a heading, but the underline emphasis is lost, as no markdown or alternative representation was applied for the underline.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Energy, Mi
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
